channel.title: healthTest
channel.description: This is a randomly ordered feed of the most recent entries from healthTest, skipping any feeds with errors.

item.title: Taking the heat. Scientists explore sauna's health benefits
item.description: It's an age old practice that's having a moment right now. But is there anything to the health claims? Scientists say sauna is more than hot air.

item.title: Maternal exposure to environmental stressors is associated with decreased odds of infant survival among preterm infants
item.description: 1. In this cohort of very preterm infants, maternal exposure to low levels of greenness, high levels of ozone, and low temperatures was associated wit...

item.title: Placebo may be inferior compared to acetaminophen when given with morphine for the management of acute pain in the emergency department
item.description: 1. Morphine plus placebo is not noninferior to morphine plus acetaminophen in the management of acute pain in the emergency department (ED). Evidence ...

item.title: FDA grants priority review for iberdomide-based myeloma regimen
item.description: 1. The FDA has granted priority review for iberdomide, a first-in-class oral CELMoD designed to degrade transcription factors more effectively than ea...

item.title: 'Dopamine Kids' explains why children crave screens and helps them enjoy life instead
item.description: A mom and science writer offers an operating manual for parents grappling with kids' screen use and cravings for sweets.

item.title: First-in-Class Tx for Dravet Syndrome Shows Early Success
item.description: A first-in-class gene-regulating therapy cut seizures by up to 91% in children with Dravet syndrome in extension studies, raising hopes for a disease-...

item.title: Cheek cells may provide clues to schizophrenia risk
item.description: A simple cheek swab could one day provide a quick and noninvasive diagnostic test for schizophrenia. A new study published in Science Advances has ide...

item.title: Physician vulnerability and authenticity: How shared stories heal [PODCAST]
item.description: Subscribe to The Podcast by KevinMD. Watch on YouTube. Catch up on old episodes! Professor of clinical psychiatry Michael F. Myers discusses his book ...

item.title: Spray-on 'immune-shield' coats transplant organs to curb rejection
item.description: A new technology has been developed to suppress immune rejection, the biggest challenge in organ transplantation, without causing systemic side effect...

item.title: GLP-1 Weight Loss Higher Among Women
item.description: Studies in a new meta-analysis show sex differences in average weight loss, but similar benefits across other sub-groups. Medscape Medical News

item.title: What Factors Raise Infection Risk in ANCA Vasculitis?
item.description: Chronic lung disease is associated with a higher risk for infection in patients with ANCA-associated vasculitis during both induction and maintenance ...

item.title: Tendon Rupture Risk Linked With GLP-1 Use in Obese Patients
item.description: 5-year findings call for physicians to balance the benefits of the medication for weight loss with the rupture risks, particularly for tendons that do...

item.title: MRI May Cut BC Deaths in High Breast Density and Risk Groups
item.description: Current guidelines on supplemental imaging for screening women with dense breasts are conflicting and must evolve say experts reacting to the new stud...

item.title: New study shows how sickle cell affects brain function
item.description: Sickle cell disease is often thought of solely as a blood disorder, but new research from the Wood Neuro Research Group provides measurable evidence t...

item.title: Administrative workforce stability: the new clinical metric for 2026
item.description: In 2025, the health care industry faced a crisis that has quietly reshaped how providers think about care: administrative workforce shortages. Clinics...

item.title: Substance use on the rise among Gen Z in their early 20s
item.description: Around seven in 10 (68%) young adults who are part of generation Z report binge drinking in the past year, with almost a third (29%) regularly consumi...

item.title: How do GLP-1 agonists affect gene expression?
item.description: GLP-1s are building a reputation as "wonder drugs." First characterized for their ability to improve insulin release and treat diabetes, the drugs wer...

item.title: Medical schools agree to expand their nutrition curriculum this fall
item.description: Health Secretary Robert F. Kennedy Jr. announced that 53 medical schools in 31 states will expand their nutrition curriculum to 40 hours. Most medical...

item.title: Can a digital tablet cut back a country's overuse of antibiotics?
item.description: Overprescribing antibiotics breeds antibiotic resistance. A new tool aims to lower a notably high rate of such prescriptions in Rwanda.

item.title: Higher sleep apnea-specific hypoxic burden may predict postoperative cardiovascular complications and mortality in obstructive sleep apnea
item.description: 1. In this cohort of patients with Obstructive sleep apnea (OSA), a higher sleep apnea-specific hypoxic burden (SASHB) was associated with a significa...

item.title: Oral remibrutinib hits Phase 3 primary endpoint for inducible hives
item.description: 1. Oral remibrutinib successfully met its primary endpoint in the Phase III RemIND trial, delivering significant symptom relief for chronic inducible ...

item.title: Pregnant women in ERs took less Tylenol after Trump autism warning
item.description: A study in The Lancet finds that pregnant women in emergency rooms used less Tylenol after President Trump said it could raise their babies' risk of a...

item.title: What HCPs across the country are saying about policy and emerging research and why your voice matters
item.description: This article is sponsored by InCrowd. Health care professionals are constantly navigating changes in policy, clinical evidence and practice expectatio...

item.title: AI in pain assessment: Balancing innovation with patient safety
item.description: In Northern California and beyond, health care systems are rapidly integrating artificial intelligence (AI) and digital tools to transform how pain is...

item.title: Winter in Islamabad: Finding hope in the daily struggle
item.description: It is very cold in Islamabad. These days, I feel overwhelmed by joblessness, the constant lack of money, an upcoming medical exam, and the slow progre...
